_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to write letters neatly and smoothly, using lower-case and upper-case letters that aren't joined together.

_source.w: We are learning to write letters neatly and smoothly, using lower-case and upper-case letters that aren't joined together.
y: Writing neatly helps other people read and understand what we've written.
Practising our handwriting makes it faster and easier so we can focus on our ideas.
Good handwriting skills give us confidence when we write stories, letters, and schoolwork.
eg: We might practise writing each letter of the alphabet on lined paper, making sure they sit on the line.
We could trace over dotted letters to help our hands learn the right movements.
We might write our name at the top of every page, trying to make it neater each time.
We could copy a short sentence from the board, focusing on keeping our letters the same size.
We might practise writing upper-case letters for the start of names and sentences.
We could have a handwriting warm-up each morning where we write a few words as neatly as we can.
